clusters.claim: The teacher shortage makes a near-term structural reorganisation infeasible.
clusters.fear: reform collapses into classroom chaos under staffing pressure
clusters.assumption: the teacher shortage will not be resolved before implementation begins
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: settled — the shortage is well documented (strong evidence)

clusters.claim: Early selective tracks sort by social status more than by ability.
clusters.fear: talented but disadvantaged children lose out to family background, not ability
clusters.assumption: current admission practice proxies status rather than ability

clusters.claim: Parents have the right to choose an academically intensive track for their child.
clusters.interest: parents seeking an academically intensive track
clusters.fear: losing the ability to shape their child's education
clusters.assumption: parental choice and equity cannot both be maximised in the current structure
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: not an empirical claim — a value question

clusters.claim: Restricting state selective tracks shifts demand to church and private schools.
clusters.interest: church/private maintainers and families who can pay
clusters.value: maintainer plurality vs. equal access to selective education
clusters.fear: state disinvestment pushes selection into fee-paying schools, worsening equity
affected: families who cannot afford private alternatives
clusters.assumption: private/church capacity can absorb the shifted demand

clusters.claim: Investing in general schools lifts the skills of the whole cohort, as Portugal showed.
clusters.interest: general-school students/teachers and employers wanting broad skills
clusters.fear: without a visible flagship result, the investment path is politically fragile and gets cut
clusters.assumption: Portugal's non-structural path transfers to the Hungarian institutional context
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: moderate — the Portugal effect is documented, transferability is contested

clusters.claim: Admission reform is a low-risk, reversible first step that builds trust for larger reform.
clusters.fear: claiming progress while avoiding the structural question entirely
clusters.assumption: a small first step actually builds trust rather than just delaying larger reform
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: a strategic/value judgment, not directly measurable

clusters.claim: The central written exam measures tutoring-driven preparation more than ability.
clusters.fear: children without private tutoring lose out systematically
affected: rural families with less tutoring access
clusters.assumption: tutoring access strongly predicts exam performance independent of ability

clusters.claim: Finland's equity gains came from a bundle (structure + teacher policy + support), not from structure alone.
clusters.interest: those skeptical of copying Finland wholesale
clusters.value: caution about international transfer vs. urgency for structural reform
clusters.fear: adopting only the structural piece wastes the reform and damages trust in reform generally
clusters.assumption: the structural component can be usefully separated from teacher-policy and support
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: moderate — the bundling effect is documented, decomposability is contested

clusters.claim: A phase-down is acceptable only with enforceable teacher guarantees.
clusters.interest: teachers facing job/role disruption from phase-down
clusters.value: reform ambition vs. protecting the workforce that must carry it out
clusters.fear: teachers lose jobs, seniority or working conditions in the transition
clusters.assumption: enforceable guarantees are politically and legally achievable within the timeline
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: not empirical — a condition-setting political claim

clusters.claim: Compensation without structural change entrenches a two-tier system while appearing to fix it.
clusters.interest: equity advocates who see compensation as avoiding real reform
clusters.value: symbolic fairness vs. removing the structural cause of inequality
clusters.fear: the two-tier system becomes permanent because visible fixing relieves reform pressure
affected: pupils remaining in the non-selective track
clusters.assumption: compensation and structural preservation are politically substitutable
clusters.empirical_uncertainty: not directly measurable — a prediction about political dynamics
